steps.title: Move from evidence to conclusion
steps.content: The researchers discovered glaciers in Antarctica that were at least 60 million years old. They then concluded that Antarctica had glaciers before its continent-wide ice sheet formed 34 million years ago. The transition must show that the conclusion follows from the discovery.

steps.title: Reject contrast transitions
steps.content: Choice B signals opposition, but the conclusion follows from the evidence. Choice C and Choice D both signal concession despite an obstacle, and no obstacle is present. The needed transition marks a logical result drawn from the discovery. The answer is A.
